summary: Unit tests (test_aggregator.py) are solid with good coverage of core parsing, aggregation, and flaky detection logic, plus proper error handling tests. However, the integration tests (test_workflow.py) reference fixture directories (fixtures/basic, fixtures/flaky, fixtures/mixed) that are completely absent from the provided code—these are critical for the act integration tests to run. The workflow structure tests are reasonable but cannot validate the full pipeline without working fixtures. Missing edge cases include: empty test files, handling directory input with mixed file types, command-line parsing errors, and various boundary conditions in flaky detection (e.g., test failed in all runs). The integration test design is ambitious but fragile: it uses string replacement to modify the workflow YAML, creates temporary git repos, and has complex temp cleanup that could fail. Test fixtures in test_aggregator.py use inline strings effectively, but the promised fixture files are never materialized. As written, only the unit tests would run; the act integration tests cannot execute without the missing fixture directories.
